source.documentName: City of Cerritos Combined Financial Program FY 2025-2026

categories.name: Community Safety
categories.tldr: Keeps our neighborhoods safe — pays for LA County Sheriff deputies who patrol our streets and the Fire Department that responds to emergencies.

lineItems.name: Sheriff Services Contract
lineItems.description: Contract with the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department for law enforcement services. Cerritos is a 'contract city' — rather than having its own police force, it partners with LA County for patrol, traffic enforcement, investigations, and community-based policing. The contract covers dedicated deputies assigned to Cerritos.
lineItems.tldr: Pays for sheriff deputies who patrol Cerritos streets and respond to 911 calls. This is the city's single largest expenditure.

source.documentName: FY 2025-26 Adopted Budget — Community Safety Department

lineItems.name: Fire Services Contract
lineItems.description: Contract with the Los Angeles County Fire Department for fire suppression, emergency medical services (EMS), and hazardous materials response. Covers staffing for fire stations serving the Cerritos community.
lineItems.tldr: Funds firefighters and paramedics who respond to fires and medical emergencies in Cerritos.

lineItems.name: Crossing Guards & Parking Enforcement
lineItems.description: School crossing guard program and parking enforcement services that keep students safe and manage traffic flow around schools and commercial areas.
lineItems.tldr: Crossing guards at schools and parking officers keeping traffic safe near the Auto Square and Los Cerritos Center.

categories.name: Public Works
categories.tldr: Maintains everything you see outside — streets, parks, trees, buildings, water systems. The largest all-funds department at $47M across all city funds.

lineItems.name: Parks & Trees Management
lineItems.description: Maintenance of 23+ city parks, 28,000+ public trees, medians, and parkways. Cerritos has been named Tree City USA for 25+ consecutive years by the National Arbor Day Foundation. Approximately 250 young trees are planted each year to replace those damaged by storms, age, or accidents.
lineItems.tldr: Cares for 28,000+ trees and 23 parks. Cerritos has been Tree City USA for 25+ straight years — a real source of community pride.

source.documentName: FY 2025-26 Adopted Budget — Parks and Trees Management

lineItems.name: Street Maintenance
lineItems.description: Maintains 115+ miles of city streets including pavement repair, pothole filling, striping, signage, and traffic signal maintenance. LED traffic signals have reduced energy use by 60%.
lineItems.tldr: Keeps 115+ miles of streets smooth and safe. The city's LED traffic signals save 60% energy compared to old bulbs.

lineItems.name: Facility Maintenance
lineItems.description: Maintains all city-owned buildings including City Hall (with its pioneering 1978 solar heating system), the Cerritos Library, community centers, and the Corporate Yard with its 350,000 kWh/year solar array.
lineItems.tldr: Keeps city buildings running — including the nation's first solar-heated City Hall (since 1978!) and the Corporate Yard solar farm.

lineItems.name: Engineering & Capital Projects
lineItems.description: City engineering division managing infrastructure design, construction oversight, and the Capital Improvement Program (CIP). FY 2025-26 CIP totals $23.0M for infrastructure improvements.
lineItems.tldr: Plans and builds city infrastructure projects — this year includes $23M in capital improvements for roads, buildings, and water systems.

lineItems.name: Vehicle Maintenance & CNG Fleet
lineItems.description: Maintains the city vehicle fleet, which increasingly uses Compressed Natural Gas (CNG). The city built a CNG fueling station in 2007, and all Athens Services waste hauling vehicles are CNG-operated. The Cerritos on Wheels (COW) transit service uses propane vehicles.
lineItems.tldr: Maintains city vehicles including our clean-fuel CNG fleet. Even our trash trucks and city buses run on cleaner fuel.

categories.name: Community & Cultural Services
categories.tldr: Runs the library, recreation programs, senior services, youth sports, community events, and the swim center — everything that makes Cerritos a great place to live.

lineItems.name: Cerritos Library
lineItems.description: The award-winning Cerritos Library serves as a community hub for learning, literacy programs, children's services, and digital resources. The building itself is an architectural landmark.
lineItems.tldr: Our nationally-recognized library — a community gathering place with books, programs, and technology for all ages.

lineItems.name: Recreation Services
lineItems.description: Youth and adult recreation programs, sports leagues, swim center operations, fitness classes, and seasonal events. Programs operate out of multiple community centers and parks.
lineItems.tldr: Youth sports, swim lessons, fitness classes, and community programs in our 23+ parks and recreation facilities.

lineItems.name: Senior Services & Community Programs
lineItems.description: Programs for Cerritos seniors at the Cerritos Senior Center at Pat Nixon Park (operating for 30+ years), plus community events, groups and clubs, and social services.
lineItems.tldr: Senior center programs, community events, and social services — connecting neighbors across generations.

categories.name: Cerritos Center for the Performing Arts
categories.tldr: Runs the CCPA — a world-class performing arts venue that brings Broadway shows, concerts, and cultural events to Cerritos. Also supports arts education programs.

lineItems.name: CCPA Operations & Programming
lineItems.description: Operating the Cerritos Center for the Performing Arts (CCPA), including performance programming, facility operations, marketing, and ticket services. The CCPA is a major cultural and economic asset for the city.
lineItems.tldr: Broadway shows, concerts, comedy, and cultural performances — bringing world-class entertainment to our community.

lineItems.name: Arts Education & Community Outreach
lineItems.description: Arts education programs, facility rentals for community events, and outreach programs that make performing arts accessible to youth and families.
lineItems.tldr: Arts education for youth and affordable community access to world-class performance space.

categories.name: Administrative Services
categories.tldr: The behind-the-scenes team — finance, human resources, technology, and procurement that keep city government running efficiently.

lineItems.name: Finance & Budget
lineItems.description: Manages city finances, budget preparation, accounting, procurement, and financial reporting. Cerritos has received the GFOA Distinguished Budget Presentation Award, recognizing excellence in financial transparency.
lineItems.tldr: Manages every dollar — the team behind this budget. They've won national awards for financial transparency.

lineItems.name: Human Resources & Technology
lineItems.description: Human resources management, employee benefits, information technology systems, and cybersecurity. Supports approximately 300+ city employees.
lineItems.tldr: Manages hiring, employee benefits, and all the technology that keeps city services running.

categories.name: Community Development
categories.tldr: Plans Cerritos's future — zoning, building permits, economic development, transit services, housing programs, and code enforcement to keep the city beautiful.

lineItems.name: Planning & Permitting
lineItems.description: Manages land use planning, zoning, building permits, and development review. Includes advance planning, current planning, and GIS services. Also oversees the General Plan that guides Cerritos's future development.
lineItems.tldr: Reviews building plans and decides how land is used — making sure new development fits our community's vision.

lineItems.name: Economic Development
lineItems.description: A newly expanded team focused on attracting and retaining businesses, managing relationships with the Cerritos Auto Square and Towne Center, and leveraging city-owned properties for economic growth.
lineItems.tldr: Attracts new businesses and supports existing ones — key to keeping sales tax revenue strong at the Auto Square and Towne Center.

lineItems.name: Code Enforcement, Transit & Housing
lineItems.description: Code enforcement (recently moved from Community Safety), the Cerritos on Wheels (COW) transit service using propane vehicles, Dial-A-Ride, and affordable housing programs.
lineItems.tldr: Keeps Cerritos beautiful through code enforcement, runs clean-fuel transit buses, and manages housing programs.

categories.name: Legislative & Administrative
categories.tldr: City Council, City Manager, City Attorney, and City Clerk — the elected officials and executives who lead Cerritos and make big decisions about our future.

lineItems.name: City Council & Elections
lineItems.description: Five elected City Council members who set policy, approve the budget, and represent residents. Includes election costs and council operations.
lineItems.tldr: Our five elected leaders who vote on the budget and make policy decisions — they represent YOU.

lineItems.name: City Manager, Attorney & Clerk
lineItems.description: The City Manager oversees all city operations and prepares the budget. The City Attorney provides legal counsel. The City Clerk manages records, public notices, and ensures government transparency.
lineItems.tldr: The people who run the city day-to-day — managing operations, legal issues, and public records.
